title: Sorting Algorithms, Stability, and Ordering Contracts · Invariant Recall
summary: Order customer records by multiple keys while preserving meaningful prior order. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a sorting laboratory with operation counts after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define total, partial, and key-based orderings including tie and missing-value policy.
Trace insertion and selection sorting invariants and identify useful input regimes.
Implement stable merge sort and account for auxiliary space and copying.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
